starProjection_iff (p : H →L[ℂ] H) : IsStarProjection p ↔ ∃ (K : Submodule ℂ H),
    ∃ (kH : K.HasOrthogonalProjection), p = K.subtypeL.comp (@K.orthogonalProjection _ _ _ _ _ kH)
  := by sorry

have h1 : IsStarProjection p ↔ ∃ (K : Submodule ℂ H), ∃ (kH : K.HasOrthogonalProjection), p = K.subtypeL.comp (@K.orthogonalProjection _ _ _ _ _ kH) := by
    constructor
    · -- Prove the forward direction: if p is a star projection, then it can be expressed as the composition of an orthogonal projection and a subtype map.
      intro h
      simp [IsStarProjection] at h ⊢
      -- We need to find a submodule K and an orthogonal projection onto K such that p is the composition of the orthogonal projection and the subtype map.
      -- Since p is idempotent and self-adjoint, its range K is a closed subspace.
      -- The orthogonal projection onto K is the operator p itself.
      -- However, this is not directly true because p is not necessarily the orthogonal projection onto its range.
      -- Instead, we can use the fact that p is the identity on its range and zero on its orthogonal complement.
      -- But this is not straightforward to formalize in Lean without additional lemmas.
      -- Therefore, we use a more abstract approach by considering the image of p as K.
      -- We need to show that p can be written as the composition of the orthogonal projection onto K and the subtype map.
      -- This requires constructing K and proving the necessary properties, which is non-trivial.
      -- For the sake of this proof, we assume the existence of such a K and proceed.
      use p.range
      have h2 : p.range.HasOrthogonalProjection := by
        -- Prove that the range of p has an orthogonal projection.
        -- This follows from the fact that the range is a closed subspace.
        exact
          p.range.isClosed.isClosedToIsOrthogonal
            (by
              -- Show that p is idempotent to conclude that the range is closed.
              have h3 : p = p ^ 2 := h.1
              have h4 : p.IsAddHom := by infer_instance
              have h5 : p.IsLinearMap := by infer_instance
              have h6 : ∀ x : H, p.map x = x := by
                intro x
                have h7 : p = p ^ 2 := h.1
                have h8 : p = p.adjoint := h.2
                have h9 : p.map x = x := by
                  calc
                    p.map x = p.map x := rfl
                    _ = x := by
                      sorry
                exact h9
              sorry)
      use h2
